title: Automatic Reassignment of Flights During Runway Closure
description: The system shall automatically reassign flights scheduled for a closed runway to an alternative runway, update their departure/arrival times with appropriate delays, and ensure separation rules are maintained.
rationale: To ensure safe and efficient rerouting of flights during runway closures, preventing potential delays and ensuring air traffic safety.
business_value: Improves operational efficiency by minimizing flight disruptions and enhancing overall safety through timely rerouting and adherence to separation rules.

source.description: As an air traffic controller, I need the system to handle runway closures by automatically reassigning affected flights to alternative runways and marking them with appropriate delays so that flights are safely and efficiently rerouted while maintaining separation rules.

acceptance_criteria: flight A123 is reassigned to an alternative runway (e.g., 27R)
flight A123's departure time is updated with an appropriate delay
separation rules between all affected flights are maintained
each flight is assigned to a suitable alternative runway based on aircraft type and current traffic
no two flights are assigned to the same runway within minimum separation time
preconditions: runway closure has been declared for runway 27L
flight A123 is scheduled to depart from runway 27L
alternative runways have sufficient capacity and configuration for the aircraft types
real-time knowledge of runway closures and available alternative runways
postconditions: flights are safely and efficiently rerouted
separation rules between all affected flights are maintained
functional_constraints: enforcement of separation rules (e.g., wake turbulence, runway spacing)
edge_cases: What if there are no available alternative runways?
How should the system handle flights that cannot be safely rerouted (e.g., aircraft too large for any alternative runway)?
What happens when multiple runways are closed simultaneously?
dependencies: Real-time runway status data from ground control or sensors
Aircraft type and current traffic information for determining suitable alternative runways
Separation rules database to ensure compliance with wake turbulence and spacing requirements

interfaces.name: Runway Status API
interfaces.description: The system interfaces with an external API to receive real-time runway closure information.

interfaces.name: Aircraft and Traffic Database
interfaces.description: The system queries this database to get the necessary aircraft type and traffic data for rerouting decisions.

interfaces.name: Separation Rules Database
interfaces.description: This database is used to enforce separation rules during flight reassignment.

test_cases.expected_result: Flight A123 is reassigned to an alternative runway and its departure time is updated accordingly. Separation rules are maintained.

test_cases.description: Multiple flights affected by a runway closure.
test_cases.expected_result: Each of the flights (A123, B456, C789) is assigned to a suitable alternative runway. No two flights are assigned to the same runway within minimum separation time.
System handles the situation gracefully, possibly rerouting flights to an external airport or delaying them until a runway becomes available. Notification sent to air traffic control team.

test_cases.description: Flight too large for any alternative runway.
test_cases.expected_result: System informs the air traffic controller and holds the flight until a suitable runway becomes available or an external airport can accommodate it.
